vulnerablePopulations: patients with limited access to specialist care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Urticaria/angioedema for >6 weeks; exclude urticarial vasculitis; evaluation guided by history; align with international urticaria guidelines

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; many rely on intermittent care

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often managed in dermatology/allergy clinics in urban areas; rural care is variable
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Second-generation antihistamines with up-dosing; short steroid bursts; omalizumab/cyclosporine for refractory cases mainly in tertiary/private settings
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Cost barriers for biologics and limited specialist access outside metros
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Affordable antihistamines and growing dermatology services in urban centers
